Package org.eventb.internal.core.ast

Interface ITypeCheckingRewriter

All Known Implementing Classes:
BindingSubstitution, BoundIdentDeclRemover, BoundIdentifierShifter, BoundIdentSubstitution, DatatypeRewriter, DefaultTypeCheckingRewriter, FormulaTranslator, SameTypeRewriter, SimpleSubstitution, Substitution
public interface ITypeCheckingRewriter
Common protocol for an internal formula rewriter which is also responsible for checking that the resulting formula is type compatible with the original formula.

The first four methods are common with interface IFormulaRewriter, but as the latter is published while this interface is not, we cannot share these declarations.

The remaining methods should perform the rewrite for the given sub-class of Formula together with the type-checking. They take one parameter if the node does not contain any children, or two parameters (the original node and a new node possibly flattened and with children already rewritten). In both cases, rewriting shall be performed on the last parameter.

Finally, there are special cases for mathematical extensions where it is not always possible to build an intermediate node (i.e., with children already rewritten to a different mathematical language) and for unary expression due to introduction of generic operators between V1 and V2. For extended expressions and predicates, and unary expressions, we instead pass part of the internal data-structures that hold the already rewritten children, rather than a complete node.

Implementation note: All rewrite methods have the post-condition that the returned formula has been built with the formula factory of this rewriter. For internal node, this happens automatically. However, for leaf nodes, care must be taken to build a copy of the node if it does not carry yet the right formula factory.

Since:
2.6
